title: USGS Earthquake Feature
description: GeoJSON Feature representing a single USGS earthquake catalog event

id.description: Unique USGS event ID (network code + event code)

place.description: Named geographic region near the epicenter

time.description: Event time in milliseconds since Unix epoch (UTC)

updated.description: Last update time in milliseconds since epoch

tz.description: Timezone offset in minutes from UTC at the epicenter

url.description: URL to the USGS event page

detail.description: URL to detailed GeoJSON for this event

cdi.description: Maximum reported intensity from DYFI community reports (1-10 MMI scale)

mmi.description: Maximum estimated instrumental intensity from ShakeMap (1-10 MMI scale)

alert.description: PAGER alert level: green, yellow, orange, or red

tsunami.description: 1 if event is in a tsunamigenic region, 0 otherwise

sig.description: Significance score (0-1000) based on magnitude, DYFI, and alert level

net.description: Network code of the authoritative data contributor

code.description: Event code assigned by the contributing network

ids.description: Comma-separated list of event IDs from all contributing networks

sources.description: Comma-separated list of contributing network codes

types.description: Comma-separated list of product types available for this event

nst.description: Number of seismic stations used to determine location

dmin.description: Horizontal distance in degrees to nearest seismic station

rms.description: Root-mean-square travel time residual in seconds

gap.description: Largest azimuthal gap between adjacent stations in degrees

magType.description: Method used to calculate the preferred magnitude (mb, ml, mw, etc.)

title.description: Human-readable title combining magnitude and location
